log_entries.thought_preview: The sample overview confirms this is a PE32 executable (29KB) tagged as a 'spreader' with an overlay. It uses Petite packer and FASM compiler. The tags suggest it's a dropper/spreader malware. I need

The IOC summary confirms 3 unique IPs, 2 domains, 2 dropped files, and 1 process created. No registry persistence keys were found. I need to investigate the network activity in detail to understand th

The network activity shows the malware downloads two executable files: 'conf.exe' from maitikio.com and 'admin.exe' from cry-havok.org. These are likely droppers or payloads. The IPs are 104.239.157.2

The dropped files show two instances of budha.exe with different SHA256 hashes, suggesting either repacking or different stages. The malware is packed with Petite and compiled with FASM. I need to inv

The process activity shows budha.exe loading various system DLLs including audio-related ones (mciwave.dll, midimap.dll, mmdevapi.dll, winmm.dll) and network APIs (ws2_32, wsock32, netapi32). The nota

The registry changes show no persistence keys, which is interesting for a 'spreader' tagged malware. The malware seems to operate in memory or uses temporary locations. I have gathered most of the ava
